title: Allowable Angular Distortion and Differential Settlement Limits
content: Differential settlement δ between adjacent footings at spacing L causes angular distortion θ=δ/L. Skempton & MacDonald limits: θ<1/500 to avoid structural damage in frame buildings; θ<1/300 for cracking in brittle infill walls; θ<1/150 for severe frame distress. Settlements are measured over time with settlement plates, digital levels, or resistive gauges anchored to deep stable benchmarks. Settlement rate >5 mm/month indicates active consolidation requiring monitoring escalation.
